players.name: Luis Mejía
players.bio: A veteran shot-stopper with 56 caps, Mejía has been Panama's reliable last line of defence for years. Now 35, the Nacional goalkeeper brings calm authority to Los Canaleros' backline at his first World Cup.
players.facts: Born just 10 days before teammate Eric Davis — the two 35-year-olds are Panama's vintage 1991 double act.

players.name: César Blackman
players.bio: An attack-minded right-back who has carved out a career in Europe with Slovak champions Slovan Bratislava. Blackman's pace and 3 international goals prove he's no ordinary defender — he arrives at his first World Cup in fine form.
players.facts: Playing in Slovakia means Blackman is one of very few Panamanians to have tasted UEFA Champions League football.

players.name: José Córdoba
players.bio: The youngest of Panama's centre-backs at 25, Córdoba has earned his stripes in the Championship with Norwich City. Composed on the ball and strong in the air, he represents Panama's defensive future — and present.
players.facts: Córdoba was born in 2001 — the same year Panama's national stadium was renovated. Poetic timing.

players.name: Fidel Escobar
players.bio: Approaching his century of caps, Escobar is one of Panama's most decorated defenders. The Saprissa stalwart brings experience, physicality, and 4 international goals from set pieces — a genuine leader in the backline.
players.facts: With 99 caps heading into the tournament, Escobar could join the 100-cap club live at a World Cup. No pressure.

players.name: Edgardo Fariña
players.bio: One of Panama's newer defensive recruits, Fariña has made a bold career move to Russia with Pari Nizhny Novgorod. Still only 24, the centre-back is earning his stripes internationally with 18 caps and growing confidence.
players.facts: Fariña plays his club football in Russia — making him one of the more geographically adventurous Panamanians around.

players.name: Cristian Martínez
players.bio: A disciplined, combative defensive midfielder who shields Panama's backline with quiet efficiency. Martínez has racked up 66 caps and plies his trade in Israel with Ironi Kiryat Shmona, giving him a truly global football CV.
players.facts: Martínez plays in the Israeli Premier League — a career path that even his GPS probably didn't see coming.

players.name: José Luis Rodríguez
players.bio: A tricky left winger with 8 international goals from 70 caps, Rodríguez brings flair and directness to Panama's attack. Plying his trade in Mexican football with Juárez, he arrives at the World Cup with something to prove on the big stage.
players.facts: With 70 caps at just 27, Rodríguez is on pace to become one of Panama's most-capped players ever.

players.name: Adalberto Carrasquilla
players.bio: A creative, box-to-box midfielder and one of Panama's most important players, Carrasquilla pulls the strings from central midfield. Now with UNAM in Mexico, his vision and energy make him the engine room of Los Canaleros.
players.facts: Carrasquilla was born on 28 November — meaning he's narrowly missed celebrating a birthday at previous tournaments.

players.name: Tomás Rodríguez
players.bio: A clinical striker who has bagged 4 goals in just 13 caps — an outstanding return. Tomás Rodríguez is the new face of Panamanian attacking football, bringing sharp instincts from his time at Costa Rican giants Saprissa.
players.facts: Four goals in 13 caps gives Rodríguez a goals-per-game ratio most strikers would envy. Watch this space.

players.name: Ismael Díaz
players.bio: Panama's number 10 and one of CONCACAF's most exciting attackers, Ismael Díaz carries the weight of expectation on nimble shoulders. With 17 goals in 57 caps, the León winger is a constant danger and Panama's biggest creative threat.
players.facts: Díaz shares his goal tally of 17 international goals with striker José Fajardo — a striking coincidence.

players.name: Yoel Bárcenas
players.bio: Panama's centurion winger, Bárcenas has over 100 caps and 10 international goals to his name. The Colón-born right winger is one of the most experienced players in the squad, still delivering pace and tricks at 32 for Mazatlán.
players.facts: Bárcenas has over 100 caps — joining a very exclusive club for a nation that only debuted at the World Cup in 2018.

players.name: César Samudio
players.bio: The experienced backup keeper with 5 caps, Samudio is a trusted squad member who keeps the pressure on the starters in training. The Honduran-based goalkeeper turns 32 during the tournament, a World Cup birthday to remember.
players.facts: Samudio's birthday falls on Christmas Day — a festive gift to a family that presumably never runs out of conversation.

players.name: Jiovany Ramos
players.bio: A solid centre-back with 23 caps and 2 international goals, Ramos brings physicality and commitment to Panama's defensive options. Playing in Venezuela with Puerto Cabello, he's a dependable squad presence with an eye for goal.
players.facts: Ramos is one of only a handful of Panamanians plying their trade in the Venezuelan football league.

players.name: Carlos Harvey
players.bio: A young, energetic defensive midfielder making waves in MLS with Minnesota United, Harvey brings pressing intensity and a surprising eye for goal with 3 strikes in 28 caps. One of Panama's most dynamic engine-room options.
players.facts: Harvey was born on 3 February 2000 — technically a Millennial, but only just. Gen Z would like a word.

players.name: Eric Davis
players.bio: With 107 caps and 9 goals, Eric Davis is one of Panama's most decorated left-backs. The veteran from Colón has served Los Canaleros for over a decade, still playing domestically at 35 — a true servant of the national side.
players.facts: Davis has more international goals than most defenders ever dream of — 9 goals from left-back is practically a striker's record.

players.name: Andrés Andrade
players.bio: A composed, technically gifted centre-back who has earned his stripes in Austrian football with LASK. Andrade's 50 caps at just 27 signal a player who has matured quickly and become a cornerstone of Panama's defensive setup.
players.facts: Andrade plays in the Austrian Bundesliga — making him one of the few Panamanians with experience of central European football.

players.name: José Fajardo
players.bio: A proven international striker with 17 goals in 68 caps, Fajardo is a physical, hard-working forward who leads the line with relentless effort. The Colón native has been a mainstay of Panama's attack and arrives at the World Cup as a key weapon.
players.facts: Fajardo shares his 17-goal tally with Ismael Díaz — Panama's twin towers of international goalscoring.

players.name: Cecilio Waterman
players.bio: A big, powerful striker known for his aerial ability and hold-up play, Waterman has 15 goals in 55 caps and remains a threat at 35. Now in Chilean football, the veteran forward brings experience and raw physicality to the Panama attack.
players.facts: Waterman was born on 13 April — making him an Aries, which suits a man who leads the line like a battering ram.

players.name: Alberto Quintero
players.bio: The grand old man of Panamanian football, Quintero boasts a remarkable 141 caps and 7 international goals. Now 38 and back home at Plaza Amador, his presence in the squad is as much about leadership and legacy as anything else.
players.facts: At 141 caps, Quintero is a Panamanian legend — he's probably attended more squad dinners than some players have played matches.

players.name: Aníbal Godoy
players.bio: Panama's most-capped player with 159 international appearances, Godoy is an extraordinary servant of Los Canaleros. The combative defensive midfielder, now with San Diego FC, has been the heartbeat of Panama's midfield for well over a decade.
players.facts: With 159 caps, Godoy is so experienced he probably remembers the first time Panama qualified for the World Cup — because he was there.

players.name: César Yanis
players.bio: A tricky right winger with 5 goals in 56 caps, Yanis provides width and creativity from the flank. Now in Chilean football with Cobresal, the 30-year-old brings valuable experience and directness to Panama's attacking play.
players.facts: Yanis plays in Chile's Atacama Desert region with Cobresal — possibly the most remote base camp for a World Cup preparation.

players.name: Orlando Mosquera
players.bio: A well-travelled goalkeeper who has made his way to the Saudi Pro League with Al-Fayha, Mosquera has 48 caps and solid big-game experience. Born on Christmas Day, the festive goalkeeper brings plenty of gifts between the sticks.
players.facts: Mosquera was born on Christmas Day 1994 — Santa delivering a goalkeeper to Panama was arguably his best gift ever.

players.name: Michael Amir Murillo
players.bio: An electric right-back who has played at the top level of European football, Murillo is one of Panama's most recognisable names. Now with Beşiktaş, his 94 caps and 9 goals make him an outstanding contributor from a position not known for scoring.
players.facts: Nine international goals from right-back is frankly absurd. Murillo attacks like a winger and defends like he enjoys it.

players.name: Azarias Londoño
players.bio: Still finding his feet internationally with 11 caps and no goals yet, Londoño is Panama's wildcard striker option. The 24-year-old at Universidad Católica is raw but exciting — the World Cup stage could be where he announces himself.
players.facts: Londoño is one of the youngest strikers in the squad at 24 — the World Cup could be his career-defining moment.

players.name: Roderick Miller
players.bio: A rugged, experienced centre-back with 50 caps, Miller has had one of the more adventurous careers in the squad — currently playing in Azerbaijan with Turan Tovuz. Dependable and physical, the 34-year-old brings veteran savvy to Panama's defence.
players.facts: Miller plays in Azerbaijan — a country most footballers couldn't locate on a map, let alone choose to play in.

players.name: Jorge Gutiérrez
players.bio: A young left-back with 18 caps, Gutiérrez has developed steadily and earned his place in the squad. Playing in Venezuela with Deportivo La Guaira, the 27-year-old brings energy and defensive solidity to Panama's options at fullback.
players.facts: Gutiérrez was born on 1 September — the first day of autumn, which is fitting for a player in the autumn of his rise to prominence.
